children.heading: 11002(a) substance which requires CERCLA notice
children.label: 1 11002(a) substance which requires CERCLA notice
children.text: If a release of an extremely hazardous substance referred to in occurs from a facility at which a hazardous chemical is produced, used, or stored, and such release requires a notification under section 103(a) of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 [] (hereafter in this section referred to as “CERCLA”) ( et seq.), the owner or operator of the facility shall immediately provide notice as described in subsection (b).section 11002(a) of this title42 U.S.C. 9603(a)42 U.S.C. 9601

children.label: A
children.text: is not a federally permitted release as defined in section 101(10) of CERCLA [],42 U.S.C. 9601(10)

children.label: B
children.text: is in an amount in excess of a quantity which the Administrator has determined (by regulation) requires notice, and

children.label: C
children.text: occurs in a manner which would require notification under section 103(a) of CERCLA [].42 U.S.C. 9603(a)

children.heading: Non-11002(a) substance which requires CERCLA notice
children.label: 3 Non-11002(a) substance which requires CERCLA notice
A
children.text: If the substance is one for which a reportable quantity has been established under section 102(a) of CERCLA [], the owner or operator shall provide notice as described in subsection (b).42 U.S.C. 9602(a)

children.label: i
children.text: Until , the owner or operator shall provide, for releases of one pound or more of the substance, the same notice to the community emergency coordinator for the local emergency planning committee, at the same time and in the same form, as notice is provided to the National Response Center under section 103(a) of CERCLA [].April 30, 198842 U.S.C. 9603(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: On and after , the owner or operator shall provide, for releases of one pound or more of the substance, the notice as described in subsection (b).April 30, 1988

children.heading: Exempted releases
children.label: 4 Exempted releases
children.text: This section does not apply to any release which results in exposure to persons solely within the site or sites on which a facility is located.

children.heading: Recipients of notice
children.label: 1 Recipients of notice
children.text: Notice required under subsection (a) shall be given immediately after the release by the owner or operator of a facility (by such means as telephone, radio, or in person) to the community emergency coordinator for the local emergency planning committees, if established pursuant to , for any area likely to be affected by the release and to the State emergency response commission of any State likely to be affected by the release. With respect to transportation of a substance subject to the requirements of this section, or storage incident to such transportation, the notice requirements of this section with respect to a release shall be satisfied by dialing 911 or, in the absence of a 911 emergency telephone number, calling the operator.section 11001(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The chemical name or identity of any substance involved in the release.

children.label: B
children.text: An indication of whether the substance is on the list referred to in .section 11002(a) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: An estimate of the quantity of any such substance that was released into the environment.

children.label: D
children.text: The time and duration of the release.

children.label: E
children.text: The medium or media into which the release occurred.

children.label: F
children.text: Any known or anticipated acute or chronic health risks associated with the emergency and, where appropriate, advice regarding medical attention necessary for exposed individuals.

children.label: G
children.text: Proper precautions to take as a result of the release, including evacuation (unless such information is readily available to the community emergency coordinator pursuant to the emergency plan).

children.label: H
children.text: The name and telephone number of the person or persons to be contacted for further information.
actions taken to respond to and contain the release,
any known or anticipated acute or chronic health risks associated with the release, and
where appropriate, advice regarding medical attention necessary for exposed individuals.

children.heading: Transportation exemption not applicable
children.label: d Transportation exemption not applicable
children.text: The exemption provided in (relating to transportation) does not apply to this section.section 11047 of this title

children.heading: Addressing source water used for drinking water
children.label: e Addressing source water used for drinking water
A
children.text: promptly notify the applicable State agency of any release that requires notice under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the applicable State agency the information identified in subsection (b)(2); and

children.label: C
children.text: provide to the applicable State agency a written followup emergency notice in accordance with subsection (c).

children.label: i
children.text: promptly forward such notice to any community water system the source waters of which are affected by the release;

children.label: ii
children.text: forward to the community water system the information provided under paragraph (1)(B); and

children.label: iii
children.text: forward to the community water system the written followup emergency notice provided under paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: Direct notification
children.label: B Direct notification
children.text: In the case of a State that does not have an applicable State agency, the State emergency response commission shall provide the notices and information described in paragraph (1) directly to any community water system the source waters of which are affected by a release that requires notice under subsection (a).

children.heading: Community water system
children.label: A Community water system
children.text: The term “community water system” has the meaning given such term in section 1401(15) of the Safe Drinking Water Act [].42 U.S.C. 300f(15)

children.heading: Applicable State agency
children.label: B Applicable State agency
children.text: The term “applicable State agency” means the State agency that has primary responsibility to enforce the requirements of the Safe Drinking Water Act in the State.
